meta.title: Koyasan Ski Area
meta.description: Wakayama's only natural snow—free snow play next to UNESCO Koyasan and Zen & Snow experiences.

hero.title:
Koyasan
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Wakayama's only natural snow—free entry and parking. A snow picnic beside a World Heritage site.

pivot.title:
Kansai's hidden
free snow field.
pivot.lead: No lifts, no snowmaking—Wakayama's only natural snow on a forest-park slope. You won't beat Rokko or Biwako Valley on facilities, but uncrowded privacy plus World Heritage context is a different game.
pivot.noticeBody: Snow is unpredictable—the top question is "is there snow today?" Check the live dashboard and the tourism association (0736-56-2616) before leaving home.
pivot.imgAlt: Koyasan snow field—children sledding near sacred forest

primary.title: World Heritage × natural snow
primary.lead: Steps from sacred Koyasan—snow-temple walks and snow play in one day. "Snow × Japanese Buddhism" for international visitors is impossible to copy elsewhere.
primary.imgAlt: Danjo Garan in snow—Koyasan winter pilgrimage

secondary.title: All-free value
secondary.lead: Rokko charges ¥2,800 plus ¥1,500 parking; Biwako Valley ¥4,000+—Koyasan is free. Low friction for first-snow families and dog owners.

camp.title:
Warm up at
shukubo · onsen.
camp.lead: Fix the top complaint—no warm food on hill—with area partners: Fukuchi-in onsen, Yadori Iyashi no Yu, Tsukumo kamameshi lunch via snow-play × warmth coupons.
camp.imgAlt: Koyasan shojin meal—warming lunch after snow play

nordic.title:
Bring a tent—
snow picnic OK.
nordic.lead: Hike-up only—parents need stamina, but tent, burner, and bento freedom beats commercial resorts. Sleds, snowmen, and dogs are the stars.
nordic.noticeBody: No lodge, rental, or regular food trucks—routing guests to nearby shops plus live snow DX raises satisfaction and area spend.
nordic.imgAlt: Snow picnic—family with tent and sleds

journey.title:
From Osaka,
one Zen & Snow day.
journey.lead: Day trip from Kansai—free morning snow play, shojin or kamameshi lunch, snowy temple walk, optional onsen finish.

01.title: Kansai region
01.body: Nankai Koya Line · cable to Koyasan

pivot.title: How to read a free hill
pivot.body: Zero direct revenue—work as a magnet and double area spend at shukubo, dining, and onsen.

nordicGuide.title: Family & dog snow guide
nordicGuide.body: Sled tips, tent etiquette, hike-up pacing—a realistic half-day with kids.

campGuide.title: Complete Zen & Snow guide
campGuide.body: Snow × Shingon sacred ground—premium international tours and flash marketing on fresh snow alerts.

access.line: 45-17 Koyasan, Koya, Ito-gun, Wakayama (Tenjikusan Forest Park)
access.note: Bus from Nankai Koyasan Cable Koyasan Sta. · alight Nakanohashi cemetery etc. · ~100 free parking
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Kansai Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Koyasan, Hashimoto, and Wakayama loops
